description:
# Stop emitting N independent pattern matches when the patterns share a discriminator

## The smell

For a multi-clause `fn` like:

    fn eval({:num, n}), do: ...        # arity 2
    fn eval({:add, a, b}), do: ...     # arity 3
    fn eval({:mul, a, b}), do: ...     # arity 3

today's `lower_multi_clause` emits **three independent** pattern
matches, each starting with its own TypeTest:

    block0: TypeTest arity-2  → block(project)  else block1
    block1: TypeTest arity-3  → block(check :add)  else block2
    block2: TypeTest arity-3  → block(check :mul)  else fail   ← same arity check, again

block2 re-loads the same HeapKind and schema_id that block1 just
loaded. The pattern lowering treats each clause as independent.

## What changes

Add a **discriminator-extraction** pre-pass over the clause list,
then emit a **shared decision tree**:

1. Group clauses by arity (or by ListIsNil, or by Atom literal —
   whatever the leading pattern discriminator is).
2. Emit one TypeTest per arity (not per clause). Clauses with the
   same arity flow into a shared post-TypeTest block, where the
   next discriminator (e.g. atom at field 0) further dispatches.
3. Build the tree as deep as the patterns let us; fall back to
   per-clause cascade only where a pattern doesn't expose a
   clean discriminator (e.g. plain wildcards, As-patterns whose
   inner is complex).

## What stays

- The clause cont-fn lift for bodies that can CPS-split
  (`body_might_cps_split`).
- Guard handling: guards run after pattern bind, can fail to the
  next "branch" (which in the tree means the next leaf, not the
  next clause).
- `lower_pattern_bind` itself for non-multi-clause sites (case,
  with) — those stay per-pattern for now (separate ticket if
  worth tackling).
- All semantics: dispatch order is preserved (clause N still
  matches before clause N+1 when both could).

## What's removed

- Cross-clause duplicate TypeTest / heap-kind load / schema-id
  load sequences.
- ast_eval's block1 vs block2 duplication (both arity 3).
- Likely 60-100 lines from ast_eval/expected.clif.
- Probably the "redundant cross-clause heap-kind checks"
  observation entirely.

## Bonus expected outcome

fz-ul4.39's investigation will tell us whether fz-i82 falls out
of this work. The hypothesis: per-callsite spec interaction
through duplicated dispatch is what causes main's CPS chain to
truncate. With shared dispatch, the per-callsite specs don't
cascade the same way. If fz-ul4.39 says yes, this ticket also
closes fz-i82. If no, fz-i82 stays as its own arc.

## Atomic steps

1. Extract `fn clause_discriminator(&Pattern) -> Option<Discrim>`
   where `Discrim` covers: tuple arity, list cons-vs-nil, atom
   literal, integer literal, type test. None for patterns
   without a clean discriminator.
2. Build the decision tree from a list of clauses; a tree node
   is either a Switch (on one discriminator) or a Leaf (run a
   clause's pattern bind + body).
3. Lower the tree to IR: one TypeTest/load per Switch node,
   shared across all clauses in that subtree.
4. Verify each existing fixture's golden either is byte-identical
   (single-clause fns; multi-clause fns where each clause has a
   distinct discriminator) or has the expected dispatch
   collapse.
5. Re-bless ast_eval golden.

## Acceptance

- ast_eval's eval CLIF emits one HeapKind load and one schema_id
  load per arity, not per clause.
- All existing fixtures pass; goldens for fns like `count`,
  `fib`, `fact`, `classify` either unchanged or strictly
  smaller.
- If fz-ul4.39 confirmed the fz-i82 attribution, fz-i82's repros
  produce correct output (closes fz-i82).
- New unit test demonstrating discriminator extraction over a
  sample multi-clause spec.

## Risk

Refactor of a core lowering path. Mitigation:
- Land conservatively: discriminator extraction returns None for
  any pattern shape we don't fully handle yet (fall back to
  cascade). Each shape can be enabled incrementally.
- May want to split into sub-tickets during implementation:
  (i) extract discriminator, (ii) build tree, (iii) lower tree,
  (iv) wire into lower_multi_clause.

## Out of scope

- Generalizing to `case` and `with` (lower_case, lower_with).
  Same shape of refactor; separate tickets if worth.
- Reordering clauses for efficiency (forbidden — would change
  semantics).
- Pattern-compile algorithms beyond a simple decision tree
  (e.g. Maranget's). Save for future work if patterns get
  complex enough to need it.

comments.text: fz-ul4.39 verdict (see fz-i82 comment): per-callsite specialization is NOT the cause of fz-i82. Decision-tree dispatch will NOT subsume it. The 'Bonus expected outcome' section in this ticket should be considered withdrawn. Scope here is now just: collapse cross-clause TypeTest duplicates and produce tighter CLIF. fz-i82 stays as its own arc.

Session handoff — design refined after .40-.44 + .37 landed.

CONTEXT: PR landed substantial wins (.40 reachability prune, .42 deletes skip_payoff_free_spec, .44 honest mode flag, .37 multi-print). This ticket is the remaining cherry — the lowering refactor itself.

CURRENT STATE OF lower_multi_clause (src/ir_lower.rs ~1030-1134):
- Per-clause try_blocks cascade
- Each clause runs lower_pattern_bind independently, emitting its own TypeTest
- Fall-through to next try_block on mismatch

REFINED V1 SCOPE (most conservative):
- Only fire when every clause's first param is Pattern::Tuple
- Group by arity; emit one TypeTest per arity group
- Within an arity group, fan out to per-clause atom-at-field-0 dispatch
- All other shapes (List, Atom, Int, Map, As, guards) → fall back to current cascade
- ast_eval qualifies; multi_clause/fib_tailrec/classify_two_clause hit fallback (goldens unchanged)

WHY v1 IS NARROW: Pattern enum has 13 variants. Doing all of them in one ticket is the original sin we're trying to avoid. Tuple-only v1 proves the structural refactor; subsequent tickets enable other discriminators.

REVISED ATOMIC STEPS:
1. Add fn first_param_tuple_arity(clause) -> Option<usize> 
2. Pre-pass: if ALL clauses return Some, partition by arity, preserving original order within partitions
3. Emit one TypeTest per arity at function entry; on success enter shared block; on failure chain to next arity test (or fail)
4. Within shared arity block, emit per-clause sub-cascade: atom check at field 0 (if Pattern::Atom), or just fall through to body (if Pattern::Var)
5. Verify dispatch ORDER preserved (clause N still beats clause N+1 — partition preserves source order within each arity group; between arity groups we must check arities in original-clause order to avoid reordering observable behavior on overlapping patterns — but tuples of different arities can't overlap, so any order between groups is safe)
6. Re-bless ast_eval golden; verify all other fixtures byte-identical
7. Acceptance test: count TypeTest sites in ast_eval CLIF == #arity-groups (2), not #clauses (3)

ALTERNATIVE CONSIDERED & REJECTED: post-lowering peephole pass (path-sensitive constant fold that detects 'block B is on false-branch of block A's same-TypeTest, so block B's TypeTest folds to false'). Cleaner in some ways (no lowering refactor) but requires building dominator + path-condition tracking infrastructure we don't have. Refactoring the lowering is more direct given our current toolset.

OPEN QUESTIONS FOR NEXT SESSION:
- Should arity-group ordering follow source-clause order (safer) or arity-ascending (cosmetic)? Recommend source order.
- For the v1 fallback predicate, do we require Pattern::Tuple at top level, or unwrap Pattern::As(_, inner) too? Recommend top-level-only for v1.
- Guard handling: a guard on a clause means after pattern-bind succeeds we may still fall through to next clause's test. With shared dispatch, 'next clause' must point into the same arity group's next leaf — verify this composes.

title: lower_multi_clause: emit shared decision tree (collapses cross-clause TypeTests)
